name: Yang Jian
description: Yang Jian, also known as Erlang Shen, is a powerful and prominent figure in Chinese mythology, particularly revered in Taoism and Chinese folk religion. Born with divine lineage, he is the nephew of the Jade Emperor, ruler of Heaven, but often operates independently, embodying a spirit of self-reliance and justice. His origins vary across different accounts, some claiming he was born from a mortal woman impregnated by a dragon, while others depict him as a celestial warrior descended from the heavens. Yang Jian is renowned for his immense strength, magical abilities, and his iconic third eye located in the center of his forehead, which grants him enhanced perception and the power to see through illusions and evil. He is often depicted wielding a three-pointed, double-edged spear and accompanied by his loyal celestial dog, Xiaotianquan (Howling Celestial Dog), which aids him in battle and hunts down demons. Yang Jian's most notable feats include aiding Wu Cheng'en's Monkey King during his rebellion against Heaven, capturing the demon dragon that plagued the mortal realm, and his unwavering commitment to upholding justice and protecting the innocent. He symbolizes strength, loyalty, and the ability to overcome adversity, making him a beloved figure in Chinese culture and a recurring character in numerous operas, novels, and films. He represents a powerful force for good, even when operating outside the direct control of the celestial bureaucracy, and his independent spirit resonates with those who value autonomy and integrity.
subtitle: The Erlang Shen, Three-Eyed Warrior
achievements: Upholding justice and protecting the innocent

enemies.name: Sometimes the Jade Emperor (depending on the story)

keyMyths.title: Yang Jian and the Lotus Lantern
keyMyths.description: Yang Jian imprisons his sister, Princess San Sheng Mu, for marrying a mortal. Her son, Chenxiang, eventually frees her, showcasing Yang Jian's strict adherence to heavenly rules, even against family. This tale highlights themes of filial piety and the conflict between divine law and human love.

keyMyths.title: Yang Jian's Aid to Sun Wukong
keyMyths.description: During Sun Wukong's rebellion against Heaven, Yang Jian is dispatched to subdue the Monkey King. Their battle is legendary, showcasing their equal strength and mastery of transformations. Ultimately, Yang Jian plays a crucial role in Wukong's capture, though with a sense of respect.

keyMyths.title: Yang Jian's Third Eye
keyMyths.description: Yang Jian's third eye grants him unparalleled vision and the ability to see through illusions. It's a symbol of his divine heritage and his ability to perceive the truth, making him a formidable opponent against demons and deceptive forces. It also allows him to fire beams of light.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
sources.description: A classic novel featuring Yang Jian in a significant role.
sources.text: A classic novel featuring Yang Jian in a significant role.
